join_order.notes: sic_ok bitset over 86K sub rows; direct lookup via num.sub_fk
Two-pass right-sized hash table ~51K entries, ~1.6MB fits L3. PARALLELIZE both passes with OpenMP.
Use tag_pk_index for O(1) lookup; check abstract[tag_row]==0 inline

data_structures.pre_eq_hashset: compact_hash_map(key=(uint32_t sub_fk, uint32_t tag_code, uint32_t version_code), value=uint32_t count, pre_sized=~51K). Two-pass build PARALLELIZED with OpenMP: pass 1 uses thread-local counters with reduction, pass 2 uses parallel insert with partitioned ranges. Memory: ~51K × 2 × 16 bytes = 1.6MB, fits L3.
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local compact_hash_map(key=uint64_t(sic,tag_row), value=(double sum, uint64_t count, vector<int32_t> cik_buf), INITIAL_CAPACITY=4096). TWO-PHASE MERGE: (1) merge all thread-local maps by integer key into single global integer-keyed map — fast O(N) integer hash ops, no strings. (2) Then iterate global map once to re-aggregate by (sic, tlabel) string — only touches each unique group once, not once per thread. This reduces string operations by ~64× vs previous approach.

parallelism.notes: Thread-local agg maps with 4096 initial capacity. build_joins phase also parallelized with OpenMP parallel for.

indexes.purpose: get EQ row range in pre

aggregation.group_key: (int16_t sic, uint32_t tag_row) packed as uint64_t
aggregation.group_key_notes: p.stmt is constant 'EQ', excluded from key. tag_row is proxy for tlabel during scan. String re-aggregation deferred to merge phase.

accumulators.cik_buf: std::vector<int32_t> — append CIK values during scan, sort+unique only at final output.

aggregation.merge_strategy: TWO-PHASE: Phase 1 — iterate all 64 thread-local maps, merge into single global unordered_map<uint64_t, AggEntry> by integer key. For CIK: move largest vector, append remaining. This is fast (integer hash only, no strings). Phase 2 — iterate the single merged integer map, read tlabel once per unique (sic, tag_row), re-aggregate into final string-keyed map. This touches each unique group exactly once instead of once per thread that observed it. Expected speedup: merge from 35ms to ~15-20ms.

optimization_notes: Category A (plan restructuring). Root cause: merge_aggregate (35ms) dominates because it performs expensive varlen string reads and string-keyed hash map operations for EVERY occupied slot across all 64 thread-local maps. Fix: two-phase merge — first merge all thread-local maps by integer key (sic, tag_row) into a single global integer-keyed map (fast, no strings), then do string re-aggregation by tlabel in a single pass over the much smaller merged result. This reduces string operations by ~64× (once per unique group vs once per thread observation). Secondary: parallelize build_joins phase — the two-pass scan over 1.24M EQ pre rows is currently single-threaded; use OpenMP parallel for with reduction for counting pass and parallel insert for the second pass. Expected improvement: merge 35→15ms, build_joins 13→5ms, total ~90ms.
